title: Getting started with autotool
text: stats::rnorm installed help; autotool pulls title per-argument descriptions straight . required list derived “default formals()” — manual second list keep sync. mean default 0 (numeric), typed "number" default propagates schema: n default R static types, falls back "string". Override schemas (shown step 4) supply better description descriptions. can also override required-arg list explicitly required = c(...) — pass character() mark every argument optional.
code: tool_rnorm <- autotool$generate_tool(stats::rnorm) tool_rnorm$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" tool_rnorm$`function`$description #> [1] "The Normal Distribution" tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$mean$description #> [1] "vector of means." unlist(tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$required) #> [1] "n" tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$mean #> $type #> [1] "number" #>  #> $default #> [1] 0 #>  #> $description #> [1] "vector of means." tool_rnorm <- autotool$generate_tool(   stats::rnorm,   descriptions = list(n = "Number of random values to draw.") ) tool_rnorm$`function`$parameters$properties$n #> $type #> [1] "string" #>  #> $description #> [1] "Number of random values to draw."

title: Getting started with autotool
text: function loaded source file (via source(), sys.source(), devtools::load_all(), box::use()), srcref attribute records file. autotool reads #' comments definition directly — roxygen2 dependency.
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(   c(     "#' Multiply two numbers together.",     "#'",     "#' @param a First factor.",     "#' @param b Second factor.",     "multiply <- function(a, b = 1) a * b"   ),   tmp ) env <- new.env() sys.source(tmp, envir = env, keep.source = TRUE)  tool_multiply <- autotool$generate_tool(env$multiply, name = "multiply") tool_multiply$`function`$description #> [1] "Multiply two numbers together." tool_multiply$`function`$parameters$properties$a$description #> [1] "First factor."

what: 3. Hiding credentials and other infra arguments
title: Getting started with autotool
text: headline feature. Suppose want expose trading function model keep api_key, base_url, retry budget pinned side. model sees symbols, start, end. Build handler make_handler() call whenever model dispatches: pinned values merged function runs. model never sees key. Plug closure handler registry (e.g., TOOL_HANDLERS <- list(fetch_quotes = handle_fetch_quotes)) dispatch name agent loop. need post-processing — caching, result truncation, logging — just write regular 1-arg function instead using make_handler(). factory convenience, requirement.
code: fetch_quotes <- function(   symbols,   start,   end = Sys.time(),   api_key = NULL,   base_url = "https://example.com",   retries = 3L ) {   return(list(symbols = symbols, start = start, end = end)) }  tool_quotes <- autotool$generate_tool(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(     api_key  = "sk-not-shown-to-model",     base_url = "https://example.com",     retries  = 5L   ),   schemas = list(     symbols = list(       type = "array",       items = list(type = "string"),       description = "Ticker symbols, e.g. ['AAPL','NVDA']"     ),     start = list(       type = "string",       description = "ISO datetime floor, e.g. '2026-04-01T00:00:00Z'"     ),     end = list(       type = "string",       description = "ISO datetime ceiling. Default: now."     )   ) ) names(tool_quotes$`function`$parameters$properties) #> [1] "symbols" "start"   "end" handle_fetch_quotes <- autotool$make_handler(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(     api_key = "sk-not-shown-to-model",     base_url = "https://example.com",     retries = 5L   ) )  # When the model emits a tool call, parse its arguments and call: model_emitted <- list(symbols = c("AAPL", "NVDA"), start = "2026-04-01") handle_fetch_quotes(model_emitted) #> $symbols #> [1] "AAPL" "NVDA" #>  #> $start #> [1] "2026-04-01" #>  #> $end #> [1] "2026-05-26 03:10:13 UTC"

title: Getting started with autotool
text: R’s lack static types means autotool infers default values falls back "string" otherwise. complex shapes — arrays--objects, nested records, custom classes — supply full schema via schemas:
code: submit_trades <- function(decisions, memo) invisible(NULL)  tool_submit <- autotool$generate_tool(   submit_trades,   description = "Submit a batch of trade decisions for execution.",   schemas = list(     decisions = list(       type = "array",       items = list(         type = "object",         properties = list(           ticker = list(             type = "string",             description = "Ticker symbol, e.g. 'AAPL'."           ),           action = list(             type = "string",             enum = list("buy", "sell", "hold"),             description = "Trade direction."           ),           notional = list(             type = "number",             description = "Dollar amount; 0 for hold."           )         ),         required = list("ticker", "action", "notional")       )     ),     memo = list(       type = "string",       description = "Short summary of this cycle's actions."     )   ) ) str(tool_submit$`function`$parameters$properties$decisions, max.level = 2) #> List of 2 #>  $ type : chr "array" #>  $ items:List of 3 #>   ..$ type      : chr "object" #>   ..$ properties:List of 3 #>   ..$ required  :List of 3

title: Getting started with autotool
text: autotool returns OpenAI / DeepSeek tool shape default. format drops straight OpenAI-compatible endpoint (DeepSeek, Mistral, vLLM, llama.cpp, etc.). ellmer, tidyverse LLM client, pass format = "ellmer" get ellmer::tool() object ready register chat session. introspection (descriptions, types, required-arg list) identical — wrapper changes. defaults still works format = "ellmer" — autotool strips hidden arguments wrapped function’s visible formals binds locals, ellmer never sees model can’t supply . format = "ellmer" covers strings, integers, numbers, booleans, enums, arrays, objects — recursively. map :
code: ellmer_tool <- autotool$generate_tool(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(     api_key  = "sk-not-shown-to-model",     base_url = "https://example.com",     retries  = 5L   ),   format = "ellmer" )  # Plug into an ellmer chat session: chat <- ellmer::chat_openai() chat$register_tool(ellmer_tool) chat$chat("Check quotes for AAPL and NVDA.")

what: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: Turn R function JSON-schema tool definition LLM can call — derived automatically function’s documentation. LLM tool calling needs schema every function want model reach. Writing schemas hand repetitive, drifts function signature, forces credentials object model sees. autotool derives schema function — formals(), installed help, roxygen comments — gives single argument (defaults) pinning anything model influence. philosophy: function already contract. model see needs fill nothing else. shouldn’t maintain second list argument names just talk LLM. Zero hard dependencies — base R packages shipped R.

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: Compare wrapping stats::rnorm model vs without autotool: description, every per-argument description, every type, required/optional flags came ?rnorm formals(rnorm). wrote one line.
code: # Without autotool — ellmer's tool() — every detail typed by hand: ellmer::tool(   stats::rnorm,   "The Normal Distribution",   arguments = list(     n = ellmer::type_integer("Number of observations..."),     mean = ellmer::type_number("Vector of means.", required = FALSE),     sd = ellmer::type_number("Vector of standard deviations.", required = FALSE)   ) )  # With autotool: autotool$generate_tool(stats::rnorm, format = "ellmer")

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: autotool CRAN; install GitHub renv:
code: # first install renv itself if you don't have it: install.packages("renv") renv::install("dereckscompany/autotool")

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: ?rnorm supplied description, title, every per-argument description. formals() supplied argument shape required list.
code: box::use(autotool)  tool <- autotool$generate_tool(stats::rnorm)  tool$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" tool$`function`$description #> [1] "The Normal Distribution" tool$`function`$parameters$properties$mean #> $type #> [1] "number" #> #> $default #> [1] 0 #> #> $description #> [1] "vector of means." unlist(tool$`function`$parameters$required) #> [1] "n"

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: Pin credentials, base URLs, implementation detail defaults — arguments disappear schema model sees: Build handler closure defaults invoke model dispatches tool call: Plug closure handler registry dispatch name agent loop. need caching, logging, post-processing, write regular 1-arg function instead — factory convenience, requirement.
code: tool <- autotool$generate_tool(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(api_key = SECRET, base_url = PROD, retries = 5L) ) # The model only sees symbols, start, ... — never api_key. handle <- autotool$make_handler(   fetch_quotes,   defaults = list(api_key = SECRET, base_url = PROD, retries = 5L) ) handle(model_args)

title: Generate LLM Tool Definitions from R Functions
text: defaults works formats — format = "ellmer" strips hidden arguments wrapped function’s visible formals automatically.
code: ellmer_tool <- autotool$generate_tool(stats::rnorm, format = "ellmer") chat <- ellmer::chat_openai() chat$register_tool(ellmer_tool)

what: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: Builds tool definition introspecting formals(fn) argument shape one two documentation backends — roxygen-via-srcref source-loaded functions, Rd-via-tools::Rd_db() installed packages — descriptions.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
code: generate_tool(   fn,   defaults = list(),   descriptions = list(),   schemas = list(),   required = NULL,   name = NULL,   description = NULL,   format = c("openai", "ellmer") )

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: fn function. Bare symbol pkg::fn work. defaults Named list arguments hide model inject call time. descriptions Named list per-argument description overrides. schemas Named list per-argument schema overrides (lists matching OpenAPI property shape). required NULL (default) auto-derive required list formals(fn) (arguments default required), character vector specifying exact required-argument names. Pass character() mark every argument optional. name Override tool name. Defaults function name. description Override tool description. Defaults parsed documentation title, stub like "Call <fn>()." documentation title explicit override available. format Output shape. One "openai" (default plain list) "ellmer" (ellmer::tool() R6 object).

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: list ("openai") ellmer tool object ("ellmer").

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: output format controls wire shape: "openai" (default) returns plain list matching OpenAI's chat-completions tool schema. Accepted -OpenAI-compatible server (DeepSeek, Mistral, vLLM, llama.cpp, etc.). "ellmer" returns ellmer::tool() R6 object ready register ellmer::chat_*() session. Requires ellmer package. introspection happens regardless format; final wrapper differs.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: headline feature defaults. argument named removed schema model sees. format = "openai", pair make_handler() merge values back dispatch model's tool call. format = "ellmer", returned tool object already wraps function defaults injected automatically.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
text: descriptions = list(arg = "...") overrides per-argument description text — useful installed-package docs terse LLM. schemas = list(arg = list(...)) overrides full schema one arguments — necessary arrays--objects nested shapes R's lack static types infer. required overrides auto-derived required-arg list: NULL (default) derives formals(); character vector replaces derived list entirely.

title: Generate an LLM tool definition from an R function — generate_tool
code: # Default OpenAI / DeepSeek shape tool <- generate_tool(stats::rnorm) tool$`function`$name #> [1] "rnorm" unlist(tool$`function`$parameters$required) #> [1] "n"  # Override the required-arg list tool <- generate_tool(stats::rnorm, required = character()) unlist(tool$`function`$parameters$required) # character(0) — nothing required #> NULL

what: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: Companion generate_tool(). Returns 1-argument closure merges supplied defaults whatever argument list LLM produced tool-call time, invokes fn. Drop closure handler registry, call model's parsed arguments.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: fn function invoke. defaults Named list pinned arguments (typically list passed generate_tool()).

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: function single argument (model_args) returns whatever fn returns.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
text: merge gives model's arguments precedence: anything model supplies key defaults overrides pinned value. usually correct — hidden arguments defaults model see, way supply — want strict pinning, validate calling. fn defaults forced factory-call time, reassigning surrounding bindings later change handler's behaviour.

title: Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults — make_handler
code: add <- function(x, y, scale = 1) (x + y) * scale handler <- make_handler(add, defaults = list(scale = 10)) handler(list(x = 2, y = 3)) #> [1] 50 # 50

title: autotool 0.0.2
text: Added hex-sticker logo. Switched standard MIT LICENSE (now detected GitHub) added citation note ORCID README.

title: autotool 0.0.2
text: generate_tool(fn, name = "x") longer hijacks source-code lookup. name argument tool name model sees; documentation lookup (roxygen via srcref, Rd via tools::Rd_db()) now always uses function’s actual source name. Previously, passing name caused autotool search source file definition matching override silently fall back "Call <name>()." stub nothing matched. Found dogfooding tradebot-mini. Source-name resolution now handles env$fn env[["fn"]] expressions addition bare symbols pkg::fn. Functions loaded via box::use() sys.source(..., envir = env) now documented correctly without needing name = "..." workaround. Anonymous functions (function(x) ... literals passed inline) now fail clear message asking name = "..." instead cryptic regex errors downstream.

title: autotool 0.0.1
text: Initial public release. generate_tool() builds LLM tool definition R function introspecting formals() pulling argument descriptions either roxygen comments (via srcref) installed-package help (via tools::Rd_db()). format argument selects output shape: "openai" (default) returns plain list matching OpenAI / DeepSeek / OpenAI-compatible server; "ellmer" returns ellmer::tool() R6 object ready register tidyverse ellmer LLM client. defaults = list(...) hides arguments schema model sees — right place pin API keys, base URLs, implementation detail. Works output formats; "ellmer" hidden arguments stripped wrapped function’s visible formals. required argument overrides auto-derived required-arg list: NULL (default) derives formals(), character vector replaces explicitly, character() marks every argument optional. descriptions = list(...) overrides per-argument description text installed docs terse. schemas = list(...) overrides full per-argument schema — required arrays--objects nested shapes R’s lack static types express. make_handler() returns 1-argument closure merges hidden defaults model’s tool-call arguments dispatches underlying function. Drop handler registry using format = "openai". Zero hard runtime dependencies — base R. ellmer, box, renv Suggests format-conversion vignette paths.
